channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Mali defence minister killed as country hit by wave of rebel attacks
item.description: Mali's defence minister was killed in a wave of rebel attacks by jihadist militants and separatists. A wave of coordinated attacks by jihadist militants and separatists has spread through the country.

item.title: Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U.S. envoys' trip canceled
item.description: Iran's foreign minister returned to Pakistan after a US envoy trip was canceled. Live Updates: Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U. S. envoys' trip canceled CBS News. Iran's foreign minister returns to Pakistan after U. S.

item.title: Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks
item.description: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi travelled to Russia for talks with senior officials. Abbas Araghchi travels to Russia, where he will speak with "senior officials", Iran's foreign ministry says. Iran’s foreign minister leaves Pakistan, heads to Russia for more talks.

item.title: Israeli settler attacks Hebron activist’s home as soldier looks on
item.description: An Israeli settler attacked Palestinian activist Issa Amro's home as a soldier looked on. An Israeli settler was filmed throwing rocks and trying to break into the home of Palestinian activist Issa Amro.

item.title: Former Israeli PMs Bennett, Lapid unite to challenge Netanyahu in elections
item.description: Former Israeli PMs Bennett and Lapid formed an alliance to challenge Netanyahu in elections. The two politicians' alliance is aimed at uniting a fragmented opposition against current PM Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: King Charles' visit to US to proceed after Washington shooting
item.description: King Charles' US visit will proceed after a shooting in Washington with increased security. Security for the Royal Family say they are cooperating with United States Secret Service during the visit.

item.title: Netanyahu's biggest rivals join forces for Israel's next election
item.description: Naftali Bennett's party merged with "There is a Future" to challenge Netanyahu's government. "Together," a merger between former right-wing Prime Minister Naftali Bennett's party and centrist "There is a Future," poses threat to Netanyahu's government in the upcoming Israeli election.

item.title: Trump and officials 'likely' targets of press dinner shooting suspect, authorities believe
item.description: Authorities believe the press dinner shooting suspect targeted Trump and officials. The suspect is expected to be charged with assault of a federal officer and using a firearm during a crime of violence, officials have said.

item.title: Trump Event Shooter Mocked Hotel Security In Manifesto, Said 'Won't Use Slugs'
item.description: Trump stated the shooter acted alone as a "lone wolf". The suspect had mocked hotel security in a manifesto. Trump said officials believed he acted alone and was what he called a "lone wolf".

item.title: Trump makes a fresh case for White House ballroom after shooting incident at dinner event
item.description: Trump contends a petitioner has no standing in a case about a White House event. Mr. Trump slammed the petitioner in the case, contending that she had absolutely no standing to bring such a suit and it must be dropped immediately.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Trade Strikes in Lebanon, as Iran Talks Remain on Hold
item.description: Israel and Hezbollah exchanged strikes in Lebanon as US-Iran talks stalled. Cease-fires in Lebanon and Iran are on shaky ground, with military attacks flaring and direct talks between Washington and Tehran to end their war stalled.

item.title: Israel’s President, Putting Off Decision on Pardon for Netanyahu, Will Push for Plea Deal
item.description: Israel's President Isaac Herzog will not pardon Benjamin Netanyahu. Instead, he will seek a plea deal in Netanyahu's corruption case. President Isaac Herzog of Israel has decided not to issue a pardon to Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in his corruption case at this time, and instead will seek mediation, officials say.

item.title: As Kash Patel's Past Catches Up, Report Hints At Trump Axe
item.description: Questions have been raised about Kash Patel's use of FBI resources. Separate questions have also been raised about Patel's use of FBI resources. As Kash Patel's Past Catches Up, Report Hints At Trump Axe.

item.title: First trial of Assad-era Syrian official starts in Damascus
item.description: Syria's first public trial of a former Assad-era official, Atef Najib, has started in Damascus. Syria begun its first public trial of former Assad-era officials, with ex-security chief Atef Najib appearing in court.

item.title: UAE-Iran differences and India’s plans to dilute language on Israel-Palestine derail joint
item.description: India hosted a BRICS deputy foreign ministers' meeting, but a joint statement couldn't be agreed upon due to differences between UAE and Iran, and India on Israel-Palestine language. A 'Chair's Statement' was issued instead.

item.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi continued diplomatic efforts over the weekend, but US President Donald Trump scrapped a visit to Pakistan by his envoys, casting doubt on US-Iran peace hopes. The stalemate risks escalating tensions without a deal.

item.title: Amid Iran's Attacks, UAE Called For Help. Israel Responded With Iron Dome
item.description: Israel deployed the Iron Dome to help the UAE counter Iran's attacks, which included 550 ballistic and cruise missiles and over 2,200 drones. The UAE had requested assistance. Iran struck the UAE with 550 ballistic and cruise missiles, and over 2,200 drones.

item.title: Trump says Iran can call US if it wants to negotiate
item.description: Trump said Iran can call the US if it wants to negotiate. Trump says Iran can call US if it wants to negotiate.

item.title: What to Know About King Charles and Queen Camilla’s U.S. State Visit Hosted by Trump
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla of Britain are set to visit the US for a state visit hosted by President Trump, with events including a garden party, a congressional address, and a banquet. The visit starts on Monday.

item.title: Notes from Central Taiwan: As Trump-Xi nears, once again, the sell out
item.description: Taiwan's government has quietly dropped plans to upgrade its military's air defense system, citing budget constraints, ahead of the US-China summit. The decision has sparked concerns among defense officials, who fear it will compromise national security.
